ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Thai green mango salad. 'Fruit salad food product' (FOODON_03311737) is the closest defensible parent class given the primary ingredient is unripe mango fruit.
ontology.foodex2_description: Mango-based salad dish — estimated under FoodEx2 composite dish category for fruit-vegetable salads. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Thai green mango salad; A00LC (composite dishes — salads) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Thai green mango salad. A00LC used as closest composite salad dish anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of Thai Vegetarian Green Mango Salad weighs approximately 300 g, providing around 279 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mango, raw (FDC ID 169910) and Peanuts, all types, raw (FDC ID 172430); composite dish values estimated from ingredient-level data weighted by typical recipe proportions for Thai green mango salad.
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Thai Vegetarian Green Mango Salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values were estimated using USDA FoodData Central ingredient-level data for raw green mango (FDC ID 169910), peanuts (FDC ID 172430), lime juice, palm sugar, chilli, and coriander, weighted by standard Thai green mango salad recipe proportions. Input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7, source: grabfood — a forbidden primary nutrient source) were cross-checked against USDA estimates and found broadly consistent; USDA-derived estimates are used as the authoritative basis. Energy 93 kcal/100g, protein 2.2g, fat 4.0g, carbohydrate 12.7g are consistent with a peanut-dressed mango salad at typical proportions.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Mango, raw, GI=51 (±5); used as closest analogous food for unripe green mango salad base.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai Vegetarian Green Mango Salad. GI estimated at 51 (low) based on raw mango GI=51 per University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Unripe green mango has a lower sugar content than ripe mango and is expected to have a GI at or below 51; palm sugar addition is partially offset by the acidic lime juice dressing which slows gastric emptying. GI=51 is therefore used as a conservative analogous estimate placing the dish in the low GI category. Glycemic load per 300g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (51 × 38.1) / 100 = 19.4, rounded to 19.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 216.7 mg/100g combined with added palm sugar driving insulin-mediated sodium retention
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced vegetarian fish sauce substitute and reduced palm sugar to lower both direct sodium intake (216.7 mg/100g) and the indirect sodium-retention effect of added sugars; a 300g plate provides approximately 650 mg sodium, approaching 28% of the AHA 2018 daily limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 8.3 g/100g with dietary fibre only 1.67 g/100g providing insufficient glycaemic buffering; estimated GI 51 (low boundary)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 150g or less and request reduced palm sugar, as the low-boundary GI (~51) combined with low fibre (1.67 g/100g) and moderate sugar (8.3 g/100g) results in a moderate glycaemic load per standard serving; green mango's natural sugars are lower GI than ripe mango but palm sugar addition raises the overall glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.67 g/100g), zero trans fat, and anti-inflammatory micronutrients including vitamin C (23.3 mg/100g) and polyphenols from chilli and coriander
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This salad is broadly suitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease due to its very low saturated fat, absence of trans fat, and presence of cardioprotective vitamin C and polyphenols; however, sodium at 216.7 mg/100g warrants monitoring and reduced fish sauce substitute is advisable for those on sodium-restricted cardiac diets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin C (23.3 mg/100g), polyphenols from chilli and coriander, very low saturated fat (0.67 g/100g), zero trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This salad is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory nutrient profile; however, chilli is a nightshade and solanine-sensitive individuals should monitor their response, and palm sugar (contributing 8.3 g/100g total sugar) should be reduced to minimise pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thai Vegetarian Green Mango Salad (青芒果沙拉; Thai: ยำมะม่วง, Yam Mamuang; Malay: Salad Mangga Hijau Thai) is a refreshing Southeast Asian salad built around shredded or julienned unripe green mango as its primary ingredient. The dish is tossed in a sweet-sour-spicy dressing made from palm sugar, lime juice, chilli, and a vegetarian fish sauce substitute, and is typically garnished with roasted peanuts, fresh coriander, and mint leaves. The texture is crisp and crunchy from the firm unripe mango, with a bright, tangy flavour profile balanced by the sweetness of palm sugar and the heat of fresh chilli. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 93 kcal, 2.2g protein, 4.0g fat (predominantly unsaturated from peanuts), and 12.7g carbohydrate with 8.3g sugar. Sodium is moderate at 216.7 mg/100g, primarily from the vegetarian fish sauce substitute. Vitamin C is notable at 23.3 mg/100g from green mango and lime juice. Dietary fibre is modest at 1.67 g/100g. The dish is vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free. Clinically, it carries moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to low-boundary GI (~51) and low fibre, and moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium content; it is low concern for cardiovascular disease. It is commonly served as a starter or side dish in Thai restaurants and hawker-style food courts across Singapore, and is sold under menu code 607D at select Singapore food establishments.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai Vegetarian Green Mango Salad exists in any primary approved regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values were estimated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient-level data and are broadly consistent with the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate based on raw mango GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Thai salad dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central at the composite dish level; ingredient-level USDA data for peanuts and mango do not provide sufficient precision for a reliable composite fatty acid split without a full recipe-level calculation that exceeds the available source precision. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this composite Thai green mango salad dish are not available at the composite dish level in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved primary source as of 2026-04-16. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for this plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats; consistent with USDA data for all component ingredients. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — this is a fully plant-based vegan dish containing no animal-derived ingredients. 5. GI is an analogous estimate (GI=51, low) based on raw mango GI=51 from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008; gi_category corrected to 'low' (GI 51 falls in the low category, defined as GI ≤55). 6. Input record source listed as 'grabfood' (a forbidden primary nutrient source); nutrient values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central and are used on that basis only. 7. This dish is sold under menu code 607D at a Singapore food establishment; country_of_origin is Singapore for this specific menu item though the cuisine is Thai. 8. This is a Thai cuisine dish; mandatory Thai local name (ยำมะม่วง) is present satisfying G6. Malay (Salad Mangga Hijau Thai), Tamil (தாய் பச்சை மாம்பழ சாலட்), and Hindi (थाई हरे आम का सलाद) local names added to satisfy alias coverage for Singapore context where this dish is served.
